home.textOne: I'm Mia Salazar, a Front-end developer, mentor, speaker and cookie monster.
home.textTwo: I started my journey in tech back in 2018 and I’ve been in love with it ever since. Today, I work as a Front-end Developer with experience in Angular, React, and Vue, and I’m currently specializing in web accessibility, a field I’m truly passionate about.
home.textThree: I’m a curious and proactive person who loves taking on new challenges and is always learning something new. I’ve been a mentor at Adalab, and I’m currently mentoring at Step4ward and TechSHEssions. I also enjoy writing articles about web development and accessibility, sharing knowledge and experiences with the community.
home.textFour: In 2024, I took another step forward and started giving talks and workshops. Since then, I’ve spoken at more than 15 events, including amazing conferences such as T3chFest, CommitConf, SalmorejoTech, and MadVue, where I also gave my first talk in English.
home.textFive: Here you can see some of the technologies I've worked with.
home.textSix: Outside of coding, I’m a geek at heart: I love watching TV shows, playing board games and RPGs, or catching a good movie at the cinema.
home.textSeven: Most of my experience is focused on Front-end development, however, back-end development is also an area that attracts my attention and in which I continue to develop.

activities.textTwo: The topics I'm most passionate about giving talks on are those related to accessibility, although I'm also drawn to talking about the value of mentoring, soft skills, and front-end development in general.
activities.textThree: These are some of the places I've been involved in
activities.textOne: In 2023, I participated in TechSessions, a CommitConf mentorship program for women that encouraged us to give talks at tech conferences. Thanks to that program, and to my mentor Marisa Martín Serrano, I started giving talks and continue to do so to this day.
activities.text: I have currently given almost 30 talks and workshops at conferences, events, and associations. Some of these workshops have been thanks to my colleagues.

contact.text: If you have something to tell me or an interesting challenge, don't hesitate to write to me!
contact.name: Name
contact.comment: What do you want to tell me?

portfolio.text: Here you can see some of the projects I've worked on professionally, and many of the personal projects I've done.
portfolio.textTwo: Before being a cookie monster, I was an intergalactic traveler and a superheroine. You can see my projects here.
portfolio.shop: The Lord of the Rings Store

collaborations.text: I really enjoy contributing to the community, which is why I've collaborated with several organizations by mentoring, participating in interviews, game jams, or volunteering.
collaborations.techThree: I was mentored in this program by Marisa Martín Serrano, and I gave 3 talks: one 10 minutes, one 15 minutes, and one 20 minutes.
collaborations.techMThree: I was a mentor in this edition, guiding a young woman in giving 3 talks.
collaborations.adalabThree: I was a mentor at Adalab for two of its cohorts, Olguin and Mileva, mentoring 3 and 2 mentees respectively for 6 months
collaborations.stepThree: I currently mentor women through this initiative and onboard new mentors. I primarily specialize in mentees who want to take their first steps in the world of technology.
collaborations.lesThree: I volunteered at the Ludo Ergo Sum (LES) board game event, performing duties such as crowd control, providing information, security, game management, and setup
collaborations.freakThree: I volunteered at the Freak Wars wargame event, handling ticket and access control, monitoring figurines, providing information, and selling merchandise
collaborations.itThree: I participated in an interview for IT Gals where I talked about what's been happening since then and how my career has evolved
collaborations.itGalsThree: I participated in an interview for IT Gals in where I talked about how I went from being an advertising expert in North Korea to being a frontend developer.
collaborations.teleThree: I was interviewed on Telemadrid talking about board games
collaborations.discoThree: I had a conversation with Disco Duro de Roer in which we talked about web accessibility
collaborations.opThree: I was interviewed on Opground about my first steps and the development of my career.

articles.text: I really enjoy writing articles on various topics. My favorite is accessibility, but I also write about front-end development, programming, and soft skills.
articles.textTwo: Here you can check all my articles.
